data.title: Activist investor Irenic took stake in Papa John's in Q3 - report
data.description:
Activist investor Irenic Capital took a stake in Papa John's during the third quarter. Papa John's rose 2%.

It wasn't immediately clear what Irenic's intentions are for Papa John's, according to a Reuters report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The size of Irenic's stake wasn't known.

Irenic declined to comment to Reuters, while Papa John's did not respond to a request for comment. 

Irenic's stake is likely to be made public when the investor files its 13F filing on Friday, the deadline for the quarterly submission to be made.

The latest update comes after Reuters reported last Tuesday that Apollo Global (APO) withdrew a $64/share takeover offer for the pizza chain.

data.title: Activist investor builds stake in Papa John's - Reuters
data.description:
• Activist investor Irenic Capital Management has accumulated a stake in pizza chain Papa John’s (PZZA), according to sources cited by Reuters.
  • The size of Irenic’s stake and its intentions for the company are currently unknown.
  • According to the company's website, Irenic "invests in change." 
  • The disclosure briefly drove Papa John’s (PZZA) shares into the green for the day before retreating.   
  • Papa John’s (PZZA) has recently been the target of takeover speculation with the latest suitor, Apollo Capital Management (APO), pulling its $64 per share offer.

data.title: Sempra raised to Buy at Goldman as increased focus on Texas drives earnings upside
data.description:
Sempra (SRE) edges higher in Friday's trading as Goldman Sachs upgraded the utility holding company to Buy from Neutral with a $106 price target, raised from $88, saying the company's increased focus on Texas should drive earnings upside in the medium term.

After lowering Sempra's (SRE) rating in February, Goldman analyst Carly Davenport said the company has seen improved management communication and execution, legislative wins including the approval of the unified tracker mechanism in Texas and the mitigation of California wildfire risk via SB 254, progress on the balance sheet through announced asset sales, and further growth in Texas, with more capex towards transmission and potential for a constructive rate case outcome in the coming quarters.

Davenport said Sempra’s (SRE) Texas utility Oncor is poised to benefit from increased data center load growth in the region, as well as higher business, infrastructure, and population growth in Texas, enabled by a constructive regulatory backdrop, with the preliminary capital
plan update at Oncor as highly supportive of upside potential, as management expects to boost capital investments by at least 30% in the Texas subsidiary, with further upside potential.

After reflecting the increased capital plan in Texas, Davenport raised Goldman's outlook for Oncor EPS growth to ~21% in 2025-29 from 15% previously, with the consolidated company’s EPS growth now at 10%-plus over the same period, compared to ~9.5% previously.

data.title: Flowserve downgraded at BofA as near-term nuclear upside now priced in
data.description:
Flowserve (FLS) -1% in Friday's trading as Bank of America downgraded shares to Neutral from Buy with a $73 price target, believing the near-term nuclear upside is priced into the shares, and weakness in the company's core end markets in oil/gas and chemicals likely extends into 2026.

60% of Flowserve's (FLS) sales are related to oil/gas and chemical markets, and key leading
indicators for these end markets continue to soften due to oil price weakness, including continued weak rig counts in 2025, according to BofA analysts led by Andrew Obin, adding that the company's organic sales lag U.S. rig counts by about five quarters.

Flowserve's (FLS) margin expansion is strong and likely will drive above-peer EPS growth in 2026, but the incrementals are largely priced in, Obin and his team also said.

While Flowserve (FLS) clearly will be a longer-term beneficiary from the nuclear opportunity, which is accretive to overall margins, BofA noted projects are long-cycle - booked out 2-3 years in advance - and bookings are lumpy.

data.title: Meta plans to assess employees on AI-driven performance next year: report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) plans to begin including how its employees utilize artificial intelligence to improve their performance when it conducts annual reviews, starting next year, according to Business Insider.

According to the report, Meta's head of people, Janelle Gale, issued an internal memo on Thursday that said employees' usage of AI to improve productivity will become a "core expectation."

"As we move toward an AI-native future, we want to recognize people who are helping us get there faster," Gale wrote.

"It's well-known that this is a priority, and we're focused on using AI to help employees with their day-to-day work," said a Meta spokesperson, according to the report.

The "move toward an AI-native" future is similar to directives issued to employees across the tech world, including Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) and Microsoft (MSFT). 

The Washington Post reported back in June that companies such as Duolingo (DUOL), Shopify (SHOP) and Box (BOX) were already requiring employees to adopt AI tools for handling day-to-day assignments.

data.title: Crypto stocks see mixed response from short sellers in October
data.description:
Crypto stocks saw mixed interest from short sellers in the month of October. While four companies saw short interest decrease, the remaining four saw it increase in the month.

Bitfarms (BITF) saw the highest jump in short interest this month to 17.31% from 8.79% last month. Short interest in Strategy (MSTR), Coinbase (COIN) and Core Scientific (CORZ) increased to 9.74%, 6.49% and 19.87% respectively.

On the other hand, short interest in MARA Holdings (MARA), Riot Platforms (RIOT) and CleanSpark (CLSK) declined to 25.59%, 14.94%, 19.89% and 5.49%.

The short dollar volume in the crypto stocks stood at $16.93 billion in October. Of these, Strategy and Coinbase attracted the most interest from short sellers, accounting for $11.43 billion of the total bets.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices remained above the $100K mark throughout October. However, in November, the cryptocurrency plunged in tandem with the broader markets, falling below the $100K level. On November 13, bitcoin closed 1.91% lower at $99,765.06, its lowest since May 7.

data.title: Tiger Global takes new stake in Netflix, cuts position in Meta in Q3 moves
data.description:
Billionaire investor Chase Coleman's Tiger Global Management picked up a new stake in Netflix (NFLX), piled up shares in Amazon (AMZN) and Block (XYZ), while significantly reduced its position in Meta Platforms (META), among third quarter moves.

Tiger Global disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing for the three months ended Sept 30, 2025, published on Friday.

The hedge fund snapped up 201,900 shares of streaming giant Netflix this quarter. Other than that, it also took a new stake of 341,000 shares in enterprise software firm MongoDB (MDB) and 60K class A shares of Figma (FIG), among others.

Meanwhile, New York-based Tiger Global exited its position in pharma giant Eli Lilly (LLY) and cybersecurity major CrowdStrike (CRWD).

Tiger Global increased its position in Amazon (AMZN) to 11.04M shares from 10.69M, while it now has 4.42M shares in Block (XYZ), increased from 3.94M. It has also hiked its stake in Broadcom (AVGO), Coupang (CPNG) and GE Vernova (GEV), the filing showed.

Looking at cuts, the firm decreased its position in Meta Platforms (META) to 2.82M class A shares from 7.53M shares, in Sherwin-Williams (SHW) to 329,175 shares from 1.2M shares, in Reddit (RDDT) to 4.67M shares from 6.15M shares.

data.title: Engaged Capital takes new position in BlackLine, Cognex, lowers stake in Evolent Health in Q3 moves
data.description:
Engaged Capital initiated a new position in BlackLine (BL) and Cognex (CGNX), increased its stake in Portillo's (PTLO), BRC (BRCC) as well as GXO Logistics (GXO), while reducing its position in Evolent Health (EVH) among its Q3 moves.

The hedge fund disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing, which was published on Friday.

The Glenn Welling-led fund acquired 453,794 shares in BlackLine worth $24.09 million and 855,000 shares in Cognex worth $38.73 million.

The fund boosted its position in BRC from 13.53 million shares to 13.93 million shares, in Portillo's from 4.83 million shares to 5.07 million shares, and in Yeti (YETI) from 2.16 million shares to 2.17 million shares.

Additionally, it added 445,000 shares to bring its position to 5.86 million in V.F. Corporation (VFC) and 81,477 shares to bring its position to 978,477 shares in GXO Logistics.

On the other hand, the hedge fund trimmed its position in Evolent Health from 5.85 million shares to 5.66 million shares and maintained its position in Freshpet (FSPT) at 619,130 shares.

data.title: Alkami Technology jumps on report activist Jana Partners took stake
data.description:
• Alkami Technology (ALKT) rose 3.2% on a report that activist investor Jana Partners took a stake in the banking technology company in Q3.
  • It's not clear whether Jana will press for changes or what its intentions are for Alkami (ALKT), according to a Reuters report, which cited people familiar with the matter.  The size of the stake wasn't known.
  • The stake may be confirmed later Friday when Jana files its quarterly 13F filing. Alkami has a market cap of about $2 billion.
  • Jana declined to comment to Reuters, while Alkami couldn't be immediately reached for comment.

data.title: Bitcoin breaks down into bear market territory, slumps to a 6-month low
data.description:
Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) decline deepened on Friday afternoon, with the world’s largest cryptocurrency sliding to a fresh six-month low as selling pressure intensified across digital-asset markets.

The token was recently trading near $95,500, down 4.2% on the day, after briefly touching an intraday trough of $94,500—its lowest level since May 6. 

The renewed weakness places bitcoin firmly in bear-market territory, now down 24.4% from its all-time high of $126,272 reached on October 6. The pullback has dampened sentiment among traders who had hoped the late-year stretch would bring renewed momentum following bitcoin’s record-setting surge earlier in 2025.

Friday's decline comes after a CoinDesk report earlier in the day said U.S.-listed spot bitcoin ETFs saw their second-largest single-day net outflows since their launch.    

Still, some market participants may find a modest source of optimism in bitcoin’s technical setup. The cryptocurrency’s relative strength index has fallen to 30.45, approaching the threshold commonly viewed as signaling oversold conditions. An RSI near or below 30 is often interpreted as a sign that bearish momentum may be nearing exhaustion, potentially setting the stage for a stabilization or short-term rebound. 

Notably, bitcoin last entered oversold territory on February 28, when it was trading near the $84,000 level. While technical signals offer no guarantees, they may provide a measure of reassurance for investors assessing whether the latest downturn is nearing a point of fatigue.

Even amid the current crypto rout, some players are still going all in. Strategy (MSTR) executive chairman Michael Saylor told CNBC on Friday that the company was buying "quite a lot" of bitcoin.      

Bitcoin ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), and (EZBC).
